title: Metatranscriptomics sample preparation protocol w/ScriptSeq
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"c7f8q","text":"This protocol is an update of the Metatranscriptomics protocol from DeLong lab 2012 which incorporates ScriptSeq v2 RNA library preparation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a37ta","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"aj4on","text":"Part A: RNA extraction, DNase treatment, purification, and quantification.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ceslf","text":"Part B: rRNA subtraction","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f85sd","text":"Part C: cDNA synthesis and Illumina library prep","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"bv4no","text":"General notes:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6nprv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3ncr","text":"*  Prior to all lab work, wipe down bench surfaces and lab equipment with RNase-Zap (Ambion) to deactivate RNases.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3mq8e","text":"*  Replace gloves frequently during the procedure.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dnj8v","text":"*  Confirm the availability of all downstream reagents before proceeding to the next step.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"82u5a","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ek74j","text":"Part A: RNA exraction, DNase treatment, purification, and quantification","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":72}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7ai5g","text":"-- for filters in 300 µl RNAlater","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6g4mc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"75mpp","text":"A1. RNA extraction","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bf1dp","text":"--- based on mirVanaTM miRNA Isolation kit (Ambion)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":13,"length":3},{"style":"sup","offset":20,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1lc7q","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5ecvk","text":"Preparation:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b44oo","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ccu7g","text":"-  Add 21 ml of ACS grade 100% ethanol to Wash Solution 1, and mix well.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fb19g","text":"-  Add 40 ml of ACS grade 100% ethanol to Wash Solution 2/3, and mix well.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"flugs","text":"-  Make sure the Acid-Phenol:Chloroform is not more than 6-months old.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"16ffa","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"65q4p","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fagdr","text":"A2. RNA quantification","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a3tq6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cln6a","text":"Depending on your application, the recommended option for RNA quantification is:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5bqje","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bkogq","text":"1.  Bioanalyzer (Agilent) using the RNA 6000 Pico total RNA kit: quantitative range = 0.05-5.0 ng/µl (dilute samples 1:10 and 1:100 prior to analysis), allows visualization of RNA size distribution, less accurate, ~1 hr processing time.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fk3m5","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4s029","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5u8nm","text":"Part B: rRNA subtraction","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":24}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9ticq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3f19m","text":"B1. PCR amplification of rRNA genes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dqi12","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7mhnr","text":"This step creates sample-specific amplicon pools using DNA template extracted from samples collected in parallel with those used to generate the total RNA extract. It is important to use DNA template that roughly mirrors the taxonomic composition of the microbial community in the samples to be subtracted. The sample-specific amplicon pools will be used as template for in vitro transcription (using T7 RNA polymerase) to produce anti-sense rRNA probes complimentary to rRNA in the total RNA extract. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2p1om","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ctq0s","text":"Primers:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"tc5r","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"doeos","text":"Bacteria","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c32uu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3f9ed","text":"16S:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"courf","text":"Eub16S_27F  ACCTGGTTGATCCTGCCAG","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c4bh2","text":"Euk18S_1520R_T7  AATTATAATACGACTCACTATAGATTCYGCAGGTTCACCTAC","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8l62g","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f9bd","text":"28S:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dm5md","text":"Euk28S_26F  ACCCGCYGAAYTTAAGCATA","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"au405","text":"Euk28S_3126R_T7  AATTATAATACGACTCACTATAGATTCTGRYTTAGAGGCGTTCAG","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7tehi","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e4ceo","text":"See DeLong et al. 1999 (AEM 65:5554-5563) for design of T7-appended primers and Hunt et al. 2006 (AEM 72:2221-2225) for bacterial 23S primers. Archaeal primers are based on conserved stretches in an alignment of taxa representing major Crenarchaeal and Euryarchaeal divisions. Eukaryotic primers are based on conserved stretches in representative marine taxa. The 5’ bases upstream of the T7 promoter facilitate RNA polymerase binding. Transcription efficiency increases if the first bases downstream of the promoter are GG in the transcribed sequences (CC in the template strand).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5cojt","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"760ut","text":"Protocol for 50 µl reaction* (using Herculase® II Fusion polymerase, Agilent 600675):","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"33tse","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"83qaj","text":"   Template DNA 100 ng Bact 23S (35 cycles) All other primers (35 cycles)   Herculase 5X buffer 10µl 95°C    2 min 95°C    2 min   dNTP (10mM) 1.25µl 95°C    20 sec 95°C    20 sec   F primer (10µM) 1.25µl 39°C    20 sec 55°C    20 sec   R primer (10µM) 1.25µl 72°C    2 min 72°C    2 min   Herculase pol 1µl 72°C    3 min 72°C    3 min   H2O To 50µl       ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":339,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":356}],"data":[]},{"key":"31o4o","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cv8v4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7nock","text":"* Modify protocol accordingly if using a different polymerase. Significant PCR optimization may be necessary for each sample type. Performing multiple reactions per sample (typically four 50 µl reactions for 16S, four 50 µl reactions for 23S, etc) and pooling the products afterwards may be necessary. High PCR yields (e.g., ~250-500 ng/µl) are needed for the next step (in vitro transcription). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ab84a","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1cdu4","text":"B2: In vitro transcription (w/ T7 RNA polymerase) of biotin-labeled antisense RNA probes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dhjhl","text":"---based on MEGAscriptTM High Yield Transcription kit (Ambion)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":22,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"74mfl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4abuq","text":"For a standard 20 µl reaction*:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fid98","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"debo3","text":"   PCR amplicons (step B1, suggest 250-500 ng)* 1µl   ATP (75 mM) 2 µl   GTP (75 mM) 2 µl   CTP (75 mM) 1.5 µl   UTP (75 mM) 1.5 µl   Biotin-11-CTP (10 mM, Roche 04739205001) 3.75 µl   Biotin-16-UTP (10 mM, Roche 11388908910) 3.75 µl   10X buffer 2 µl   SUPERase•InTM RNase inhibitor 0.5 µl   T7 RNA polymerase 2 µl   ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":318}],"data":[]},{"key":"biglj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"eqgdv","text":"*Use Speedvac to concentrate PCR amplicons down to 250 ng/µl","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3aumg","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8ugqk","text":"* The IVT reaction is linear and therefore dependent on starting DNA concentration. A 4-6 hr reaction with 250-500 ng of starting template typically yields high probe concentrations (e.g., 1500-2000 ng/µl). If yield is low, the reaction volume can be increased to 40 µl. Assuming ~50% GC, ~1 in 8 nucleotides should be biotin-labeled in the resulting aRNA probes. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9ce29","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cj0ac","text":"B3. rRNA subtraction with biotinylated aRNA","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"11ge3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"220b9","text":"This step binds biotinylated aRNA probes (step B2) to rRNA in the total RNA sample. The labeled ds rRNA is then removed via hybridization to streptavidin-coated magnetic beads (NEB S1420S), followed by magnetic separation (via a separation rack; e.g., DynaMagTM-Spin Magnet, Invitrogen 123-20D).  Depending on the rRNA concentration in the sample, the ratio of probe to template RNA may need to optimized. To start, we recommend a probe-to-RNA ratio of 2:1, and a reaction volume of 50 µl in 1X sodium chloride-citrate (SSC) and 20% formamide.  For bacterioplankton samples, we’ve found that \u003e 20% formamide inhibits probe-bead binding, and \u003c 20% allows non-specific binding (non-target RNA to beads).  The procedure obviously involves a significant reduction in RNA concentration. Depending on downstream applications (e.g., RNA amplification), we recommend using as much starting RNA template as possible (ideally 250-500 ng) however we can usually generate enough subtracted RNA for ScriptSeq using as little as 100 ng starting RNA template. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":259,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"133ev","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cr7io","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"215jc","text":"Part C: cDNA synthesis and Illumina library prep","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":48}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"eohq2","text":"---based on EpiCenter ScriptSeq v2 RNA-Seq library preparation kit","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4g2hd","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ea5dl","text":"Notes: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"85p90","text":"-  Remove all components except enzymes and Finishing solution, allow to thaw at RT and store on ice. Centrifuge briefly before use. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"10c9t","text":"-  A thermal cycler with heated lid is recommended for fragmentation and PCR ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"efad7","text":"-  For cDNA synthesis steps, leave lid of PCR machine open, use Styrofoam lid instead ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8or1t","text":"-  Otherwise, follow standard ScriptSeq protocol, reproduced below","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"39ecc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2de5d","text":"C1. Fragment RNA and anneal cDNA synthesis primer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4l0u9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4j485","text":"C2. Synthesize cDNA","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2n6md","text":"Note: Use sytrofoam lid on thermocycler, not heated lid for C2-C3","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"508b8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d60i9","text":"Thermocycler setting for part C2:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1bua7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1fs5a","text":"25°C for 5 minutes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a03mv","text":"42°C for 20 minutes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bpgvh","text":"37°C Pause","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bu3ge","text":"37°C for 10 minutes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"340vf","text":"95°C for 3 minutes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"aupdl","text":"25°C Pause","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2t4ft","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bs26f","text":"C3. Terminal-tag the cDNA","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e7n74","text":"Note: Use sytrofoam lid on thermocycler, not heated lid for C2-C3. Thoroughly mix the terminal-tagging premix before use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dpuo4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5aeeq","text":"Termocycler settings for Part C3:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dgklk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7qg94","text":"25°C for 15 minutes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4rbkm","text":"95°C for 3 minutes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"es5ce","text":"4°C hold or ice","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cvlp1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"86jij","text":"C4. Purify the cDNA","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3q0tg","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2a6nq","text":"C5. Amplify the library and add an index","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4tjgq","text":"Notes: Typically, 12-15 cycles of PCR are performed, more can be done to increase library yields if needed. For 500 pg-5ng input, do 15 PCR cycles. To add an index, replace the Reverse PCR primer included with this kit with one of the ScriptSeq Index PCR primers, available from EpiCenter. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bhmnj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b6mds","text":"C6. Purify the RNA-Seq library","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"auicd","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5hrbs","text":"At this point, the samples are ready for pooling (check forcompatible indices) and sequencing according to the latest MiSeq sequencing protocol. For v2 sequencing kits we recommend a final pooled library concentration of 6 pM to get a 800K cluster density.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":256}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"tables","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"cellsMeta":{"0_0":"","0_1":"","0_2":"","0_3":"","1_0":"","1_1":"","1_2":"","1_3":"","2_0":"","2_1":"","2_2":"","2_3":"","3_0":"","3_1":"","3_2":"","3_3":"","4_0":"","4_1":"","4_2":"","4_3":"","5_0":"","5_1":"","5_2":"","5_3":"","6_0":"","6_1":"","6_2":"","6_3":""},"colWidths":[null,null,null,null],"data":[["Template DNA","100 ng","Bact 23S (35 cycles)","All other primers (35 cycles)"],["Herculase 5X buffer","10µl","95°C    2 min","95°C    2 min"],["dNTP (10mM)","1.25µl","95°C    20 sec","95°C    20 sec"],["F primer (10µM)","1.25µl","39°C    20 sec","55°C    20 sec"],["R primer (10µM)","1.25µl","72°C    2 min","72°C    2 min"],["Herculase pol","1µl","72°C    3 min","72°C    3 min"],["H\u003csub\u003e2\u003c/sub\u003eO","To 50µl"," "," "]],"guid":"D0935370457F11E99ACDFF15730CFDB3","legend":"","mergeCells":[],"rowHeights":[],"show_headers":true}},"1":{"type":"tables","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"cellsMeta":{"0_0":"","0_1":"","1_0":"","1_1":"","2_0":"","2_1":"","3_0":"","3_1":"","4_0":"","4_1":"","5_0":"","5_1":"","6_0":"","6_1":"","7_0":"","7_1":"","8_0":"","8_1":"","9_0":"","9_1":""},"colWidths":[null,null],"data":[["PCR amplicons (step B1, suggest 250-500 ng)*","1µl"],["ATP (75 mM)","2 µl"],["GTP (75 mM)","2 µl"],["CTP (75 mM)","1.5 µl"],["UTP (75 mM)","1.5 µl"],["Biotin-11-CTP (10 mM, Roche 04739205001)","3.75 µl"],["Biotin-16-UTP (10 mM, Roche 11388908910)","3.75 µl"],["10X buffer","2 µl"],["SUPERase•InTM RNase inhibitor","0.5 µl"],["T7 RNA polymerase","2 µl"]],"guid":"D0952830457F11E99ACDFF15730CFDB3","legend":"","mergeCells":[],"rowHeights":[],"show_headers":true}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"85amq","text":"*  Prior to all lab work, wipe down bench surfaces and lab equipment with RNase-Zap (Ambion) to deactivate RNases.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7frbu","text":"*  Replace gloves frequently during the procedure.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"boigg","text":"*  Confirm the availability of all downstream reagents before proceeding to the next step.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Thaw 2-ml screw-cap tube on ice (should take a while). If using sterivex filters, follow notes in annotations. Preparation: - Add 21 ml of ACS grade 100% ethanol to Wash Solution 1, and mix well. - Add 40 ml of ACS grade 100% ethanol to Wash Solution 2/3, and mix well. - Make sure the Acid-Phenol:Chloroform is not more than 6-month old.
Lyse the cells on the filters: Remove the 300 µl RNAlater carefully and discard.
Add 750 µl (1.5mL)* Lysis/Binding buffer, vortex vigorously to completely lyse the cells. *If using sterivex filters: add 1.5mL

Add 75 µl (150 µl)* miRNA Homogenate Additive. Vortex to mix. *If using sterivex filters: Add 150 µl Homogenate Additive: 2M sodium acetate, PH 4; the sodium acetate buffer helps maintain the pH of the denatured cell lysate during acid extraction and provides the salt necessary for RNA precipitation later.
Leave the mixture on ice for 10 min. 00:10:00

Transfer the 825 µl lysate to a new Non-stick RNase-free tube or a 2 mL tube with o-ring (might need to spin the tubes to get as much lysate out as possible). If using sterivex filters, split lysate equally into two tubes.
Add 750 µl Acid-Phenol:Chloroform, vortex for 30-60 sec to mix. 00:01:00
Centrifuge for 5 min at maximum 10,000 X g at RT. 00:05:00 After centrifugation, the interphase should be compact, if not, repeat centrifugation.
Carefully remove the top aqueous phase w/o disturbing the lower phase, transfer it to a fresh tube. The volume of the aqueous phase should be similar to initial volume of Lysis/Binding buffer.
Pre-heat Elution Solution (or Nuclease-free water) to 95ºC.
Add 1.25 volumes of RT 100% ethanol to the aqueous phase, mix well, load to a Filter Cartridge in the collection tube (provided).
Centrifuge for 15 sec at 10,000 X g, discard the flow-through. 00:00:15
Apply 700 µl miRNA Wash Solution 1 to the Filter Cartridge and centrifuge for 10 sec at 10,000 X g, discard the flow-through. 00:00:10
Apply 500 µl Wash Solution 2/3, spin 15 sec at 10,000 X g, and discard the flow-through. 00:00:15
Repeat step 14.
Put the Filter Cartridge back in the tube, centrifuge for 1.5 min at 10,000 X g to remove residual fluid from the filter. 00:01:30
Transfer the Filter Cartridge into a fresh collection tube, apply 50 µl pre-heated (95 ºC) Elution Solution (0.1 mM EDTA) to the center of the filter.
Wait ~1 minute. 00:01:00
Spin for 30 sec at 10,000 X g. 00:00:30
Repeat steps 17-19 with another 50 µl pre-heated (95ºC) Elution Solution.

Add 1/10th volume of DNase I buffer (~10 µl) and 2 µl DNase I (2U/µl), gently mix and incubate at 37 ºC for 30 min. 00:30:00 Preparation : set heat block or hyb oven to 37C before starting this section.
If RNeasy MinElute is to be used immediately after this, there is no need to use the DNase inactivation reagent – go directly to the RNeasy MinElute Procedure.
Add 0.1 volume (10 µl) of DNase inactivation reagent, mix well, incubate at RT for 2 min, flicking the tube occasionally. 00:02:00
Centrifuge for 1.5 min at 10,000 X g, transfer RNA to a fresh new 0.5 ml non-stick Rnase-free tube, store in –70 ºC. 00:01:30
Pre-heat elution buffer or water to 40°C. Preparation : add 4 volumes of ethanol (100%) to buffer RPE before starting this section.
Combine two 100 µl DNase-treated RNA samples in a 2ml Nonsticky RNase-free tube (~200 µl total). Adjust starting volumes accordingly depending on sample - see kit instructions.
Add 700 µl RLT buffer, and mix with by pipeting. Adjust starting volumes accordingly depending on sample - see kit instructions.
Add 500 µl 100% ethanol, mix thoroughly by pipetting. DO NOT CENTRIFUGE.
Immediately load 700 µl onto an RNeasy MinElute Column in a 2 ml tube, close the tube gently, centrifuge for 15 s at ≥ 8,000 X g. Discard flow through. 00:00:15
Repeat step 29 to process the remaining sample volume through the column.
Transfer the spin column to a new 2 ml collection tube.
Pipet 500 µl buffer RPE onto the column, close tube gently.
Centrifuge for 15 s at ≥ 8,000 X g to wash. 00:00:15
Discard the flow-through. Reuse the collection tube.
Add 500 µl of 80% ethanol to the column, close tube gently.
Centrifuge for 2 min at ≥ 8,000 X g, discard flow-through. 00:02:00
Transfer the column to a new 2 ml collection tube.
OPEN the cap of the tube, and centrifuge at 12,000 X g for 5 min, discard flow-through. 00:05:00
Elute with 25 µl pre-heated nuclease-free water or RNA storage buffer.
Transfer the spin column to a new 1.5 ml tube, pipet water or buffer directly onto the center of the silica-gel membrane, wait for 2 min at RT, close gently. 00:02:00
Centrifuge for 1 min at 12,000 X g to elute. 00:01:00
Repeat elution using 15 µl of water or buffer.
Store elutions at -80°C or continue with quantification.

Depending on your application, the recommended option for RNA quantification is: Bioanalyzer (Agilent) using the RNA 6000 Pico total RNA kit: quantitative range = 0.05-5.0 ng / µl (dilute samples 1:10 and 1:100 prior to analysis), allows visualization of RNA size distribution, less accurate, ~1 hr processing time.

Order primers (found in guidelines). This section creates sample-specific amplicon pools using DNA template extracted from samples collected in parallel with those used to generate the total RNA extract. It is important to use DNA template that roughly mirrors the taxonomic composition of the microbial community in the samples to be subtracted. The sample-specific amplicon pools will be used as template for in vitro transcription (using T7 RNA polymerase) to produce anti-sense rRNA probes complimentary to rRNA in the total RNA extract.

PCR amplify rRNA. %s %s Template DNA 100 ng Bact 23S (35 cycles) All other primers (35 cycles) Herculase 5X buffer 10µl 95°C 2 min 95°C 2 min dNTP (10mM) 1.25µl 95°C 20 sec 95°C 20 sec F primer (10µM) 1.25µl 39°C 20 sec 55°C 20 sec R primer (10µM) 1.25µl 72°C 2 min 72°C 2 min Herculase pol 1µl 72°C 3 min 72°C 3 min H2O To 50µl Modify protocol accordingly if using a different polymerase. Significant PCR optimization may be necessary for each sample type. Performing multiple reactions per sample (typically four 50 µl reactions for 16S, four 50 µl reactions for 23S, etc) and pooling the products afterwards may be necessary. High PCR yields (e.g., ~250-500 ng/µl) are needed for the next step (in vitro transcription). This is for 50 µl reaction* (using Herculase® II Fusion polymerase, Agilent 600675):

Purify PCR products using the QIAquick PCR purification kit (Qiagen)** with elution in 50 ul elution buffer and quantify DNA concentration using the Nanodrop (Nanodrop is fine here, as the PCR products should be at high conc. (>100 ng/µl)) **The Herculase buffer changes the pH of the PB buffer in this kit. You will need to add 10 µl of sodium acetate to each PB+PCR product mix to change pH indicator back to yellow.

Prepare separate reactions for all probes. For a standard 20 µl reaction*: %s %s PCR amplicons (step B1, suggest 250-500 ng)* 1µl ATP (75 mM) 2 µl GTP (75 mM) 2 µl CTP (75 mM) 1.5 µl UTP (75 mM) 1.5 µl Biotin-11-CTP (10 mM, Roche 04739205001) 3.75 µl Biotin-16-UTP (10 mM, Roche 11388908910) 3.75 µl 10X buffer 2 µl SUPERase•InTM RNase inhibitor 0.5 µl T7 RNA polymerase 2 µl *Use Speedvac to concentrate PCR amplicons down to 250 ng/µl * The IVT reaction is linear and therefore dependent on starting DNA concentration. A 4-6 hr reaction with 250-500 ng of starting template typically yields high probe concentrations (e.g., 1500-2000 ng/µl). If yield is low, the reaction volume can be increased to 40 µl. Assuming ~50% GC, ~1 in 8 nucleotides should be biotin-labeled in the resulting aRNA probes.
At room temperature (not on ice, as spermidine in the reaction buffer may cause DNA precipitation), mix reagents in the order listed.
Incubate at 37ºC for 4-6 hrs. Recommended: use a thermocycler, or up to overnight depending on yield.
After incubation, add 1 µl DNase I (included in the MEGAscript kit) to remove the DNA template.
Incubate at 37ºC for an additional 30 min. 00:30:00

Purify synthesized RNA using the MEGAclear TM kit, with elution in 50 µl elution solution. The MEGAclear kit is designed for recovery of high amounts of product and is therefore ideal for IVT reactions (recommended here over the RNeasy kit).
Quantify RNA concentration using either RiboGreen or Nanodrop (Nanodrop should work fine as RNA samples should be at high concentration).
Store probes at -80°C.
Bead washing (do this before or during the hybridization step). For each sample, transfer 400 µl of beads* to a 1.5 ml tube. *Vortex beads before use and before measuring at each step

Wash 1: bind beads to magnetic separation rack (takes ~2 min), pipet off and discard supernatant, re-suspend beads in equal volume of 0.1N NaOH (deactivates bead-associated RNases), and quick vortex to re-suspend, spin down, re-bind to magnet and pipet off supernatant. Do this step quickly.
Wash #2: Repeat wash in step57 using 1X SSC buffer. On the 3rd wash, leave beads in buffer on ice until hybridization is complete.
Wash #3: Repeat wash in step57 using 1X SSC buffer and leave beads in buffer on ice until hybridization is complete.

Hybridization. For a 50 µl reaction*, prepare in a PCR tube: %s %s Template, total RNA (ideally 250-500 ng in <36.5 ul) X µl aRNA 16S probe (500-1000 ng)** X µl aRNA 23S probe (500-1000 ng)** X µl SUPERase•In RNase inhibitor 1 µl 20X Sodium chloride-citrate (SSC) buffer (RNase free) 2.5 µl Formamide (100%)*** 10 µl Nuclease-free water (if necessary) To 50 µl * Template and probe volumes will be scaled depending on concentrations. ** To avoid pipetting small volumes, make a diluted probe master-mix (100 ng/µl) prior to starting. ***To prevent formamide degradation, store small aliquots (1 ml) at -20°C
In a thermal cycler, incubate under the following conditions: 5 min at 70ºC Rampdown to 25°C using 5°C increments for 1 min each.
Remove the reaction and let sit at RT for 2-5 min. 00:05:00
Bead binding. While the hybridization reaction is at RT, capture the pre-aliquoted beads (400 µl per sample) on the magnetic rack, pipet off the supernatant, and remove the beads from the rack.
Dilute the hybridization reaction in PCR tubes to 100 µl using 20% formamide in 1X SSC.
Add the hybridization reaction (now 100 µl) to the dried beads.
Incubate at RT for 10 min , with occasional flicking to mix. 00:10:00
Bead removal. Quick spin the tubes. Capture the beads on the magnetic rack (2-3 min).
Transfer the non-rRNA-containing supernatant to a 1.5 ml tube using a P200 pipettor.
Re-suspend remaining beads with 100 µl 1X SSC, capture beads as above, transfer supernatant to the same 1.5 ml tube (200 µl total volume).
Purify subtracted RNA (200 µl) to remove formamide. Use the RNeasy MinElute kit. Elute in 15 µl and 5 µl pre-heated to 50 °C (combine the elutions).
Run 1 µl of purified RNA (diluted 1:10 and 1:100) on the Bioanalyzer to confirm rRNA subtraction and probe removal. Not absolutely necessary, but recommended.
Store RNA at -80°C for downstream applications.

If needed, speedvac the subtracted RNA to maximum volume of 9 µl. Use all of the template material remaining after the rRNA-subtraction step. - Remove all components except enzymes and Finishing solution, allow to thaw at RT and store on ice. Centrifuge briefly before use. - A thermal cycler with heated lid is recommended for fragmentation and PCR - For cDNA synthesis steps, leave lid of PCR machine open, use Styrofoam lid instead - Otherwise, follow standard ScriptSeq protocol, reproduced below

In a 0.2 mL PCR tube assemble the following reaction: %s %s Nuclease-free water x µl rRNA-subtracted RNA y µl RNA fragmentation solution 1.0 µl cDNA synthesis primer 2.0 µl Total reaction volume 12 µl
Fragment RNA by incubation at 85 ºC for 5 minutes in thermocycler with heated lid. 00:05:00
Stop fragmentation reaction by placing the tube on ice.

On ice preare the cDNA synthesis master mix, volumes below are per rxn: %s %s cDNA synthesis premix 3 µl 100 mM DTT 0.5 µl StarScript Reverse Transcriptase 0.5 µl Total volume 4 µl Thermocycler setting found in guidelines. Use sytrofoam lid on thermocycler, not heated lid for C2-C3
Gently but thoroughly mix the cDNA synthesis master mix by pipetting.
Add 4 µl of the cDNA synthesis master mix to each reaction on ice from Part C1 and mix by pipetting.
Incubate at 25 °C for 5 minutes followed by 42 °C for 20 minutes.
Cool the reactions to 37 °C and pause the thermocycler.
Remove one reaction at a time from the thermocycler, add 1.0 µl of Finishing solution and mix gently by pipetting. Return each reaction to the thermocycler before proceeding to the next.
Incubate at 37 °C for 10 minutes. 00:10:00
Incubate each reaction at 95°C for 3 minutes, then cool reactions to 25 °C and pause the thermocycler. 00:03:00
During the 95 °C incubation prepare the terminal tagging master mix described in C3.

On ice, prepare the terminal tagging premix: For each reaction, combine on ice: %s %s Terminal tagging premix 7.5 µl DNA polymerase 0.5 µl Total volume 8 µl Thermocycler setting found in guidelines. Thoroughly mix the terminal-tagging premix before use.
Remove one reaction at a time from the thermocycler (25 °C paused) and add 8 µl of the terminal tagging master mix.
Gently mix by pipetting. Return each reaction to the thermocycler before proceeding to the next.
Incubate each reaction at 25 °C for 15 minutes. 00:15:00
Incubate each reaction at 95 °C for 3 minutes. Then cool to 4 °C on ice or in thermocycler. 00:03:00
Use the Agencourt AmpureXP system to purify the cDNA with 1.8X purification.
Warm Ampure beads to room temperature for 30 minutes. 00:30:00
Prepare 400 µl fresh 80% ethanol at room temperature for each sample.
Add 45 µl of beads to each microfuge tube containing the di-tagged cDNA from part 3C.
Mix thoroughly by pipetting 10 times.
Transfer volume to 1.5 mL tube.
Incubate at room temp for 15 minutes. 00:15:00
Place tubes in magnetic stand at room temp for at least 5 minutes until liquid clear. 00:05:00
Remove and discard supernatant using a pipet, some liquid may remain in tube.
Ethanol Wash #1: With tubes on stand, add 200 µl 80% ethanol to each tube without disturbing the beads.
Ethanol Wash #1: Incubate for 30 seconds, then remove and discard supernatant. 00:00:30
Ethanol Wash #2: With tubes on stand, add 200 µl 80% ethanol to each tube without disturbing the beads.
Ethanol Wash #2: Incubate for 30 seconds, then remove and discard supernatant. 00:00:30
Allow tubes to air-dry on magnetic stand for 15 minutes at room temp. 00:15:00
Add 24.5 µl of nuclease-free water to each tube and remove from magnetic stand.
Thoroughly resuspend beads by pipetting 10 times.
Incubate tube at room temperature for 2 minutes. 00:02:00
Place the tube on magnetic stand at room temp for at least 5 minutes, until liquid clears. 00:05:00
Transfer 22.5 µl supernatant, which contains di-tagged cDNA, from each tube to a new 0.2 mL PCR tube, place on ice.

In a 0.2 mL PCR tube containing 22.5 µl of di-tagged cDNA from C4, add on ice: %s %s FailSafe PCR Premix E 25 µl Forward PCR primer 1 µl Reverse or ScriptSeq index primer 1 µl FailSafe PCR Enzyme (1.25 U) 0.5 µl Total Volume 50 µl Typically, 12-15 cycles of PCR are performed, more can be done to increase library yields if needed. For 500 pg-5ng input, do 15 PCR cycles. To add an index, replace the Reverse PCR primer included with this kit with one of the ScriptSeq Index PCR primers, available from EpiCenter.

Perform PCR: Denature ds DNA at 95 °C for 1 minute Followed by 12-15 cycles of: 95 °C for 30 seconds 55 °C for 30 seconds 68 °C for 3 minutes Finish with 68 °C for 7 minutes Bring thermocycler temperature down to 4 °C
After PCR complete, proceed immediately to purification step C6.
Use the Ampure XP system to purify the PCR reaction to remove the primer-dimers that can occur during PCR.
Warm Ampure beads to room temperature for 30 minutes. 00:30:00
Prepare 400 µl fresh 80% ethanol at room temperature for each sample.
Add 50 µl of beads to each tube containing amplified library from part C5.
Mix thoroughly by pipetting 10 times.
Transfer each 100 µl volume to 1.5 mL tube.
Incubate at room temp for 15 minutes. 00:15:00
Place tubes in magnetic stand at room temp for at least 5 minutes until liquid clear. 00:05:00
Remove and discard supernatant using a pipet, some liquid may remain in tube.
Ethanol Wash #1: With tubes on stand, add 200 µl 80% ethanol to each tube without disturbing the beads.
Ethanol Wash #1: Incubate for 30 seconds, then remove and discard supernatant. 00:00:30
Ethanol Wash #2: With tubes on stand, add 200 µl 80% ethanol to each tube without disturbing the beads.
Ethanol Wash #2: Incubate for 30 seconds, then remove and discard supernatant. 00:00:30
Allow tubes to air-dry on magnetic stand for 15 minutes at room temp. 00:15:00
Add 20 µl of nuclease-free water to each tube and remove from magnetic stand.
Thoroughly resuspend beads by pipetting 10 times.
Incubate tube at room temperature for 2 minutes. 00:02:00
Place the tube on magnetic stand at room temp for at least 5 minutes, until liquid clears. 00:05:00
Transfer the supernatant, which contains the RNA-Seq library, from each tube to a new tube.
Make 1:10 and 1:100 dilutions for Bioanalyzer analysis of average library size.
Perform Picogreen to get accurate quantification of library concentration.

At this point, the samples are ready for pooling (check for compatible indices) and sequencing according to the latest MiSeq/NextSeq sequencing protocol. For v2 sequencing kits with the MiSeq and NextSeq, we recommend a final pooled library concentration of 6 pM and 1.0 pM respectively.
creator: Paul Den Uyl
